name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI cloud infrastructure company that builds large-scale GPU clusters and operates an AI-native cloud platform for training, deploying, and running AI models and applications. Rebranded from Yandex N.V. after selling its Russian assets for $5.4 billion in 2024, the company operates data centers across Europe and North America, backed by a $2 billion NVIDIA equity investment, a $19.4 billion Microsoft contract, and a $27 billion Meta Platforms agreement. Headquartered in Amsterdam with a $49.5 billion market capitalization.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): The transformation from Yandex N.V. — Russia's dominant internet company — into a pure-play Western AI cloud infrastructure provider within 18 months is one of the most dramatic corporate reinventions in tech history; Q1 2026 revenue of $399M grew 684% YoY [1][2]. The company signed a $27B agreement with Meta Platforms in March 2026 and a $19.4B contract with Microsoft in September 2025, validating the business model at hyperscale [2]. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS the AI product — it operates GPU clusters for AI training and inference, with core AI cloud ARR surging 54% QoQ to $1.92B in Q1 2026 [1]. The company acquired agentic search company Tavily for ~$400M and launched a Robotics Simulation cloud with NVIDIA [2]. Market Share (1/2): Nebius is gaining share rapidly as Europe's premier pure-play AI neocloud, with NVIDIA designating it a preferred provider, but it remains a fraction of the size of AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud [2]. Pipeline (2/2): Priority access to NVIDIA's Vera Rubin NVL72 platform launching H2 2026, contracted power exceeding 4 GW by year-end 2026, plus 1.2 GW AI factories under construction in Pennsylvania and Missouri and a 310 MW Finland site [2][3]. R&D Intensity (1/1): CapEx guidance of $20-25B for 2026 on ~$3B revenue represents extraordinary infrastructure investment intensity, far exceeding any peer as a percentage of revenue [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius articulates its purpose as delivering 'the infrastructure, tools, and services required to support AI innovation at scale,' with the tagline 'Build and scale faster on the purpose-built AI cloud, engineered from silicon to API' [4]. This is clear, specific, and differentiated from generalist cloud providers, but the mission is less than two years old (2024 rebrand) and has not been validated through multi-decade execution or survived a major crisis in its current form. Community Impact (0/2): No specific charitable programs, foundation, or community investment initiatives were identified in web research. The company's sustainability page exists but focuses on operational environmental practices rather than community giving [5]. Employee Retention (0/2): Nebius has aggressively recruited top talent from Silicon Valley and European tech — Marc Boroditsky (ex-Cloudflare) as CRO, John Boynton as Chairman — but the company is less than two years old in its current form and no employee tenure, voluntary turnover, or retention data is publicly available [3]. Insider Buying (0/2): Net insider selling across the board. Over the last 6 months, insiders made 0 purchases and 20 sales, with net selling of $11M. CEO Volozh sold 33,358 shares ($3.46M) in April 2026 to cover tax obligations on RSU vesting; Director Elena Bunina sold 10,894 shares cutting her stake by over 50%; CFO Sanchez sold 1,509 shares in June 2026 [6].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available yfinance metrics. Q1 2026 delivered $399M revenue (up 684% YoY) with GAAP net income of $621M boosted by a $780.6M noncash ClickHouse revaluation gain [1]. Adjusted EBITDA surged to $129.5M with a 45% margin, up from negative $53.7M in Q4 2025 [1]. Operating cash flow was $2.3B driven by customer prepayments [1]. The profit margin of 93.1% reported by yfinance reflects GAAP figures inflated by the noncash revaluation — the underlying operating margin is significantly lower. D/E of 132.4% is elevated by the $4.3B convertible notes but manageable given the capital-intensive business model. ROE of 14.1% is just below the 15% target. Revenue growth of 683.9% YoY is extraordinary. Forward P/E of 615.25 scores at floor (0.0), reflecting speculative valuation on a pre-profitability growth trajectory. Full-year 2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B revenue with ~40% adjusted EBITDA margin [1]. Note: Forward PEG and 5yr CAGR unavailable, scored on 5 available metrics only. Liquidity check passes: $222.24 x 18,023,841 avg daily volume = $4.01B, far exceeding the $1M threshold.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Nebius IS the AI infrastructure — it provides the GPU clusters, cloud platform, and tools that AI companies use to build and run models. AI disruption is structurally impossible for a company whose entire business is enabling AI. The $27B Meta agreement and $19.4B Microsoft contract validate Nebius as a foundational AI infrastructure provider that the largest AI developers depend on [1][2]. NVIDIA's $2B equity investment and designation of Nebius as a preferred provider further cement this position [2]. Every incremental dollar spent on AI development globally increases demand for Nebius's services. AI Opportunity (1/1): 100% of Nebius's revenue comes from AI infrastructure services; core AI cloud ARR reached $1.92B in Q1 2026, up 54% QoQ and representing explosive monetization of the AI buildout cycle [1].

infinite_game.summary: Arkady Volozh's decision to renounce Russian citizenship, sell Yandex's entire $5.4B Russian business, and rebuild as a Western AI infrastructure company is one of the most audacious acts of existential flexibility in recent business history. The Nebius story is defined by a Just Cause — building the infrastructure layer that powers global AI — pursued with founder-level conviction, massive personal sacrifice, and long-term capital commitments that prioritize future capability over current profitability. The company has not yet been tested by a prolonged downturn, but the pattern of leadership behavior is deeply infinite.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius's Just Cause — building the infrastructure layer that enables the global AI industry — is authentic and validated by extraordinary personal sacrifice from founder Arkady Volozh, who renounced his Russian citizenship and divested $5.4B in Yandex assets to pursue this vision [2][3]. The purpose is clearly distinctive from competitors and shapes every strategic decision (CapEx commitments, partnership selections, geographic expansion). However, the cause is less than two years old in its current articulation and has not been tested through a multi-decade period or survived a major downturn. Volozh's leadership philosophy of 'balancing clarity with adaptability' reflects genuine long-term orientation [3]. A score of 3/3 requires decades of validated execution — Nebius earns 2/3 for the depth of conviction demonstrated but the brevity of execution history.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The Nebius leadership team has been deliberately assembled from top-tier global tech companies — Marc Boroditsky (ex-Cloudflare) as CRO, John Boynton as Chairman, and experienced operators across engineering and operations [3]. Volozh's return as CEO provides founder-led stability. However, the organization is less than two years old in its current form, with approximately 2,500 employees. There is no publicly available data on voluntary turnover, average tenure, or cultural practices. The company has disclosed material weaknesses in internal controls over fixed assets and TripleTen revenue recognition as of December 2025, which raises questions about organizational maturity and process discipline [5]. The rapid scaling from a small team to a multi-billion-dollar operation introduces inherent organizational risk.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Nebius explicitly positions itself against AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud — not to beat them on breadth, but to offer a superior specialized environment for AI workloads. The company's 'neocloud' positioning acknowledges hyperscaler strengths in general-purpose cloud while carving out a differentiated proposition based on technical depth, purpose-built AI infrastructure, and vertical specialization [2]. Management's competitive language consistently focuses on 'what developers actually need' rather than market share destruction. Volozh's framing of competition as motivation to build better purpose-built infrastructure exemplifies Sinek's worthy rivals concept.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The Nebius transformation from Russia's dominant internet company (Yandex) to a Western AI infrastructure provider is the textbook example of existential flexibility. Volozh and the board made the decision to sell everything Russian — $5.4B in assets, decades of accumulated market position, the country's leading search engine — to pursue a Just Cause aligned with the global AI buildout [2][3]. This was not a forced pivot from desperation; Yandex was profitable and dominant in Russia. The decision was driven by the belief that building AI infrastructure for the world was a larger purpose than maintaining a Russian internet conglomerate. This ranks alongside Apple's Mac-to-iPhone pivot and Fujifilm's film-to-healthcare transformation in Sinek's framework.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): Multiple examples of leadership making costly long-term bets. The $20-25B CapEx commitment for 2026 while the company is only now reaching adjusted EBITDA profitability represents an enormous bet on future demand [1]. The $4.3B convertible note raise dilutes current shareholders but funds capacity that won't generate revenue until 2027-2028. The Tavily acquisition at ~$400M invested in agentic AI capabilities before the market fully valued them. Volozh's personal sacrifice — renouncing citizenship, walking away from Russia's dominant internet franchise — is the ultimate act of courage in service of a Just Cause. The company raised its CapEx guidance from $16-20B to $20-25B after Q1 2026, doubling down on long-term capacity in the face of near-term uncertainty [1].

investment_thesis: Nebius is the purest play on AI infrastructure outside of NVIDIA itself — a company that grew Q1 2026 revenue 684% YoY to $399M, achieved $1.92B in core AI cloud ARR (up 54% QoQ), and swung adjusted EBITDA from negative $53.7M to positive $129.5M with a 45% margin in a single quarter [1]. The business model is validated by the highest-quality customer contracts in the AI ecosystem: $27B from Meta, $19.4B from Microsoft, and a $2B equity investment from NVIDIA [2]. Founder-CEO Arkady Volozh's transformation of Yandex into a Western AI infrastructure company — backed by personal sacrifice and $5.4B in divested Russian assets — demonstrates conviction and strategic clarity that few CEOs can match. With $3.0-3.4B in 2026 revenue guidance, priority access to NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform, and 4+ GW of contracted power capacity, Nebius is positioned as a structural beneficiary of the multi-trillion-dollar AI infrastructure buildout cycle. Operating cash flow of $2.3B in Q1 2026, driven by customer prepayments, provides near-term funding visibility [1].
thesis_continued: The bear case is formidable. The Forward P/E of 615.25 and $49.5B market cap on $399M quarterly revenue reflect a valuation that requires flawless execution for years. The $20-25B 2026 CapEx guidance — roughly 7x projected revenue — requires continuous capital raises that will dilute shareholders; the $4.3B convertible note raise already creates significant overhang [1]. Material weaknesses in internal controls over fixed assets and TripleTen revenue recognition as of December 2025 raise governance concerns for a company managing billions in infrastructure assets [5]. Customer concentration is extreme: Meta and Microsoft contracts represent the vast majority of contracted demand, and loss or delay in either would devastate revenue projections. Net insider selling of $11M over the past 12 months, with zero insider purchases, suggests executives are not adding to positions at current prices [6]. The GAAP profit margin of 93.1% is distorted by a $780.6M noncash ClickHouse revaluation gain — the underlying business is only now reaching adjusted EBITDA breakeven. Competition from hyperscalers scaling their own AI infrastructure, NVIDIA GPU supply constraints, and energy grid bottlenecks create execution risks that could impair the growth trajectory [5].
key_catalysts: Full-year 2026 revenue hitting $3.0-3.4B with sustained 40%+ adjusted EBITDA margins would validate the business model's unit economics and likely trigger institutional re-rating from speculative growth to established AI infrastructure platform
NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 priority deployment in H2 2026 provides next-generation hardware advantage, enabling Nebius to offer capabilities that competing neoclouds cannot access on the same timeline

Completion of 1.2 GW AI factory capacity in Pennsylvania and Missouri, combined with 4+ GW total contracted power by year-end 2026, creates physical infrastructure moat that capital-light competitors cannot replicate
key_risks: Extreme valuation risk: Forward P/E of 615.25 and $49.5B market cap require revenue scaling to $3B+ with margin expansion — any miss against guidance or slowdown in AI CapEx spending by hyperscaler customers could trigger 50%+ drawdown given speculative positioning

Customer concentration and capital structure: Meta ($27B) and Microsoft ($19.4B) contracts represent dominant revenue sources, while $20-25B CapEx funded partially by dilutive convertible notes creates dependence on continuous capital market access at favorable terms

Governance and execution immaturity: Material weaknesses in internal controls disclosed for FY2025, dual-class share structure with Volozh family trust holding 52% voting power, and less than two years of operating history in current form create institutional risk for a company deploying tens of billions in infrastructure capital
